verdict: Retargeting spend is winding down with zero visible outcomes — you're flying blind on a $814 investment.
diagnosis: Spend dropped to $10.75 on the latest day from a $24.66 daily average, with no lead, booking, or organic data to confirm results.

signals.headline: Total spend hit $813.77 this period
signals.detail: One Facebook retargeting campaign carried the entire paid budget across 28 days. No diversification, no backup channel.

signals.headline: Daily spend collapsed 56% to $10.75
signals.detail: Latest day sits well below the $24.66 daily average. The campaign appears to be tapering, not scaling.

signals.headline: Zero lead or booking data tracked
signals.detail: No CRM pipeline, organic, or local data flowing in. Impossible to judge if the $813.77 produced any members.

signals.headline: 100% of budget on retargeting
signals.detail: Retargeting only works if a top-of-funnel audience exists. Without prospecting, the warm pool will dry up fast.

signals.headline: Google Business Profile shows no activity
signals.detail: Missing local presence data means calls, direction requests, and map views are invisible. Likely a tracking gap, not zero demand.

benchmark.label: Benchmark
benchmark.body: Boutique fitness studios on Facebook typically pay $25–$50 per lead, with the category averaging around $29.70. Without lead tracking, your $813.77 spend can't be measured against this.

seasonal.label: Seasonal
seasonal.subheader: May is the cheapest month for fitness leads
seasonal.body: Fitness lead costs hit their low in April and stay cheap through May, running up to 64% below global averages. You're advertising in the best window of the year.

competitor.label: Category
competitor.body: Boutique operators are pulling back from acquisition-heavy models in 2026. Cancellations at studios dropped 6% last year while gyms saw an 8% rise — retention is winning.

recommendations.title: Connect lead and booking tracking immediately
recommendations.detail: Consider wiring Facebook leads into a CRM and connecting Google Business Profile. Without this, you cannot tell if $813.77 produced one member or twenty.

recommendations.title: Test a small prospecting campaign alongside retargeting
recommendations.detail: Retargeting alone shrinks the audience over time. Worth exploring a $15–$20/day prospecting campaign to refill the top of the funnel while costs are seasonally low.

recommendations.title: Add a qualifying question to the lead form
recommendations.detail: Fitness lead forms attract tire-kickers. Test one question like current workout frequency or budget to filter for serious prospects worth the sales follow-up.
